title: Digital Holography (off-axis and in-line intensity holograms)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is extracted from mine_example/holography.md and canonical Goodman / Kreis holography references.

E.description: A coherent reference wave R interferes with the object wave O at the sensor, yielding intensity I = |O|^2 + |R|^2 + O*R + OR*. In off-axis holography a tilted reference shifts the object term to an isolated Fourier quadrant; in in-line holography (phase-shifting or Gabor) a sequence of phase-shifted reference states isolates the complex object term algebraically.
E.world_state_x: Complex object field O(x,y) in C^{H x W} (amplitude + phase), typically related to a sample by Fresnel propagation U(x,y,z)
E.observation_y: Intensity hologram I(x,y) in R^{H x W} (photon counting; optionally N phase-shifted frames I_1..I_N)
physical_parameters_theta: R(x,y): reference wave (plane wave for off-axis, stepped-phase for in-line)
pixel size dx, numerical aperture NA
N_phase_shifts (in-line only): number of phase-shifted frames

W.regime: Off-axis single-shot: ill-posed without spatial bandwidth separation (tilt must exceed twice max object spatial frequency). In-line phase-shifting: well-posed with N >= 3 phase steps; stability dominated by reference-phase calibration error and wavelength drift.

C.solver_class: Fourier demodulation (off-axis), phase-shifting algorithm (in-line), Gerchberg-Saxton, TV-ADMM for joint denoising, deep-hologram (DH-Net)
C.error_bound: |phase_hat - phase|_L2 <= 2*pi*(dphi_ref + delta_z / z_R) + sigma/sqrt(photon_count); Fresnel propagation error scales with (lambda * dz / dx^2)
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(H*W)) per frame (2D FFT)
